task_response.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (incompletely addressed; no conclusion; ideas limited and underdeveloped)

coherence.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (organisation evident but not wholly logical; central topic of Body 1 unclear)
coherence.justification: Three paragraphs are present so the bolded Band-5 limiter "paragraphing inadequate or missing" does not strictly apply, but Body 1 has no clear topic sentence about mathematics, and the central topic drifts (preservation → tools → Mesopotamia → Morse code → "a computer"). References are repeatedly unclear: "**that** shone during the period", "**That** strong will can be seen", "**By doing that**, it can calculate" — the antecedent of each "that" is ambiguous. Per Cotton et al. (cited in `01-band-descriptors/examiner-perspective.md` §11), examiners weight paragraph-internal logic heavily, and on that proxy this script reads as Band 5.

lexical.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 ("risk-taker" clause — wider range with higher inaccuracy)
lexical.justification: Topic vocabulary is real ("electronic signals", "binary digits", "base-60", "hardware") so the resource is "generally adequate". However the risk-taker pattern produces collocation/word-choice errors at noticeable density: "**new logics**" (uncountable misuse), "**mathematics is inevitable**" (wrong collocation — should be *indispensable*), "**the first ever information**" (wrong noun — should be *message system / signalling system*), "**a single hardware**" (uncountable). This is the textbook Band 6 risk-taker description.

grammar.descriptor_evidence: 01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (complex sentences attempted but faulty; greatest accuracy on simple sentences)
grammar.justification: Complex sentences are attempted but break: "**Computers consist of a full of small switches**" (broken article + uncountable), "**so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed**" (missing auxiliary, missing plural), "**a machine which can do several things in a single hardware**". The most accurate sentences are the simple ones ("This was developed even further"), which is the Band-5 GRA signature: "the greatest accuracy is achieved on simple sentences". Errors do, in two places, cause the reader to pause — a Band-5 indicator from the same descriptor.

overall_rationale: Average 5.25, rounded up to 5.5 per the .25 → .5 rule in `01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md` §3. The vocabulary risk-taking holds LR at 6, but missing thesis + missing conclusion + faulty complex sentences keep TR/CC/GRA at 5 until structural repair lands.
what_changed: The prior calibration (5/5/6/5 → 5.5) holds — I re-tested each criterion against the descriptors and the band judgments fit the visible script. Three concrete sharpenings: (a) the structural diagnosis is now anchored on a *missing thesis* and *missing conclusion*, which together cap TR at 5 — that was implied but not stated as a single root cause in the prior; (b) two Band-7 grammar showcases (participle clause, cleft) replace the prior's safer rewrites in the table, per `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md`; (c) the focus areas are reordered so that completing the essay shape outranks vocabulary, which is the real band-uplift order for this candidate.

rewrites.original: In this essay, I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science.
rewrites.improved: This essay argues that two mathematical foundations — Boolean logic and binary representation — were decisive in shaping modern computing.
rewrites.reason: Replaces metacomment with a thesis naming concrete mechanisms; gating fix for TR.

rewrites.improved: Taken together, the reduction of letters, images and instructions to numerical values shows that mathematics did not merely assist computer science — it provided the very language in which computation became possible.
rewrites.reason: Adds the missing conclusion (synthesis + restated position) — required to clear Band 5 TR.

rewrites.original: Computers consist of a full of small switches which can turn on and off.
rewrites.improved: A computer is, at its core, an array of countless switches, each capable of holding only one of two states.
rewrites.reason: Repairs broken article ("a full of"), adds complex noun phrase as subject — a Band 7 GRA signature.

rewrites.original: If it has billions and trillions of switches, then it can go up to almost countless numbers.
rewrites.improved: Combined into the billions, these switches allow a computer to represent and manipulate values of almost arbitrary size.
rewrites.reason: Replaces vague conditional with a participle-clause opener — explicit Band-7 grammar showcase.

rewrites.original: The reason it matters is because every letter is actually a number, and every emoji is also a number.
rewrites.improved: It is precisely this numerical encoding — of letters, images and even instructions — that turns abstract mathematics into a working machine.
rewrites.reason: Cleft sentence emphasises the mechanism; corrects "the reason… is because" redundancy.

rewrites.original: so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed
rewrites.improved: so that messages no longer needed to be carried by people or pigeons
rewrites.reason: Fixes missing auxiliary and the singular "dove"; restores natural collocation.
Replaces a collocation error with a precise band-7 collocation.

rewrites.original: People have built up mathematical rules by defining new logics to solve problems.
rewrites.improved: Over centuries, mathematicians have built systems of formal logic that turn vague problems into precisely solvable ones.
rewrites.reason: Fixes "new logics" (uncountable), names the mechanism (formal logic), adds defining relative clause.

rewrites.original: the first ever information which only used electronic signals
rewrites.improved: arguably the first system to encode language as electrical pulses alone
rewrites.reason: Replaces "information" (wrong noun) with the right concept; hedges with *arguably*.

rewrites.original: By doing that, it can calculate in binary digits.
rewrites.improved: By switching between these two states, the machine performs calculations in binary.
rewrites.reason: Fixes the unclear "that" reference (`05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md`); adds technical precision.

convergence_note: The harness reports an empty prior chain, so per the rules I must output REFINING regardless of confidence. Substantively, future iterations could still tighten the rewrite #5 cleft and reconsider whether LR should drop to 5 once the risk-taker errors are weighed against the genuine topic-vocabulary hits — that boundary call has not yet been stress-tested across iterations.
raw_markdown:
# Iteration 06 — claude

_Generated 2026-05-10T10:20:24Z. Prior chain depth: up to 3. Manifest: 462 files._

---I have enough corpus to refine. The prior chain is empty per the harness, so this must report REFINING regardless. The on-disk prior at `14-real-essays/feedback/W1-mathematical-influence.md` is the working baseline; I'll sharpen it.

## Per-criterion scores
| Criterion | Band | Descriptor evidence (file path) |
|---|---:|---|
| Task Response | 5.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (incompletely addressed; no conclusion; ideas limited and underdeveloped) |
| Coherence and Cohesion | 5.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (organisation evident but not wholly logical; central topic of Body 1 unclear) |
| Lexical Resource | 6.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 ("risk-taker" clause — wider range with higher inaccuracy) |
| Grammatical Range & Accuracy | 5.0 | `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 5 (complex sentences attempted but faulty; greatest accuracy on simple sentences) |
| **Overall** | 5.5 | (5+5+6+5)/4 = 5.25 → 5.5 per `01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md` §3 |

## What changed from prior feedback
The prior calibration (5/5/6/5 → 5.5) holds — I re-tested each criterion against the descriptors and the band judgments fit the visible script. Three concrete sharpenings: (a) the structural diagnosis is now anchored on a *missing thesis* and *missing conclusion*, which together cap TR at 5 — that was implied but not stated as a single root cause in the prior; (b) two Band-7 grammar showcases (participle clause, cleft) replace the prior's safer rewrites in the table, per `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md`; (c) the focus areas are reordered so that completing the essay shape outranks vocabulary, which is the real band-uplift order for this candidate.

## Structural feedback
The essay does not have a Task 2 shape: there is **no thesis** in the introduction (the closing sentence "I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science" is a metacomment, not a position) and **no conclusion paragraph**. Per `01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 7, "a clear and developed position" is gating — this script cannot reach Band 7 TR until both ends are repaired. Body 1 wanders chronologically through Mesopotamia → Egypt → Babylonians → Morse code → "a computer", but its **topic sentence is about preserving thoughts to communicate**, not about mathematics, so the central topic is unclear (a Band 6 CC limiter; here it slides to 5 because Body 1's topic is genuinely ambiguous). Body 2 contains the only mathematically substantive content (binary representation), but it stops mid-explanation. **The single highest-impact fix is structural, not lexical:** rewrite the intro with a thesis naming 2 mechanisms (e.g. *Boolean logic* and *binary representation*), give each mechanism its own PEEL paragraph per `05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md` §3, and add a 2-sentence synthesis conclusion. Doing only this — without changing a single word of vocabulary — would already lift TR and CC to Band 6.

## Focus areas
- **Complete Task 2 shape (intro thesis + 2 PEEL bodies + conclusion)** — without this, TR is locked at 5 regardless of vocabulary. Drill: `05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md` §2–§5, `05-band-6-to-7/top-30-fixes-band-6-to-7.md` Tier 1 #1–#3.
- **Naming the actual mechanism (Boolean logic, algorithms, binary)** — the essay gestures at "math → computers" without naming any mathematical concept. Drill: `10-task2-topic-banks/topic-03-technology.md`, `10-task2-topic-banks/topic-17-science-and-research.md`, `09-instructor-tips/09-topic-vocabulary-clusters.md`.
- **Articles and countability with abstract/uncountable nouns** — "new logics", "the first ever information", "a single hardware" are all article/countability errors that block Band 7 LR. Drill: `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §A2, §B2, `08-vocabulary-grammar/01-grammar-error-correction-bank.md`.
- **Reference and substitution beyond "that" / "by doing that"** — every demonstrative in the essay has an unclear antecedent. Drill: `05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md`, `09-instructor-tips/08-cohesion-upgrades-instructor.md`.
- **Error-free complex sentences (relative + participle clauses)** — currently most complex sentences contain at least one error. Drill: `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §2, §4, `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §7–§8.

## What's working
The candidate is a "risk-taker" in the Band 6 LR sense (`01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6) and that is worth preserving. Genuinely good moves: the dated, specific "**Morse code in 1844**", the technical "**base-60 for timekeeping**", and the conceptually correct "**every letter is actually a number, and every emoji is also a number**" — that last sentence is, in essence, the right thesis for the whole essay and should be promoted to the introduction. Keep this instinct for concrete examples; what's needed is the surrounding scaffolding, not different examples.

## Per-criterion justification
**Task Response (5):** No thesis ("In this essay, I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science" is a metacomment, not a position) and no conclusion paragraph. The prompt asks about *the influence of mathematics on the development of computer science*, but Body 1's topic ("Humans have always wanted to preserve their thoughts somewhere to communicate") is about communication history, not mathematics. Band 5: "main parts of the prompt are incompletely addressed… position is expressed but the development is not always clear… ideas put forward but limited and not sufficiently developed" — this fits exactly.

**Coherence & Cohesion (5):** Three paragraphs are present so the bolded Band-5 limiter "paragraphing inadequate or missing" does not strictly apply, but Body 1 has no clear topic sentence about mathematics, and the central topic drifts (preservation → tools → Mesopotamia → Morse code → "a computer"). References are repeatedly unclear: "**that** shone during the period", "**That** strong will can be seen", "**By doing that**, it can calculate" — the antecedent of each "that" is ambiguous. Per Cotton et al. (cited in `01-band-descriptors/examiner-perspective.md` §11), examiners weight paragraph-internal logic heavily, and on that proxy this script reads as Band 5.

**Lexical Resource (6):** Topic vocabulary is real ("electronic signals", "binary digits", "base-60", "hardware") so the resource is "generally adequate". However the risk-taker pattern produces collocation/word-choice errors at noticeable density: "**new logics**" (uncountable misuse), "**mathematics is inevitable**" (wrong collocation — should be *indispensable*), "**the first ever information**" (wrong noun — should be *message system / signalling system*), "**a single hardware**" (uncountable). This is the textbook Band 6 risk-taker description.

**Grammatical Range & Accuracy (5):** Complex sentences are attempted but break: "**Computers consist of a full of small switches**" (broken article + uncountable), "**so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed**" (missing auxiliary, missing plural), "**a machine which can do several things in a single hardware**". The most accurate sentences are the simple ones ("This was developed even further"), which is the Band-5 GRA signature: "the greatest accuracy is achieved on simple sentences". Errors do, in two places, cause the reader to pause — a Band-5 indicator from the same descriptor.

## Top concrete rewrites (5–10, ranked by band uplift)
| # | Original phrase from essay | Improved (band-7 target) | Reason | Corpus citation |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "In this essay, I will discuss how mathematics influenced computer science." | "**This essay argues that two mathematical foundations — Boolean logic and binary representation — were decisive in shaping modern computing.**" | Replaces metacomment with a thesis naming concrete mechanisms; gating fix for TR. | `05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md` §2, `09-instructor-tips/06-task2-sentence-frames.md` |
| 2 | (no conclusion paragraph) | "**Taken together, the reduction of letters, images and instructions to numerical values shows that mathematics did not merely assist computer science — it provided the very language in which computation became possible.**" | Adds the missing conclusion (synthesis + restated position) — required to clear Band 5 TR. | `05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md` §5 |
| 3 | "Computers consist of a full of small switches which can turn on and off." | "**A computer is, at its core, an array of countless switches, each capable of holding only one of two states.**" | Repairs broken article ("a full of"), adds complex noun phrase as subject — a Band 7 GRA signature. | `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §5, `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §7 |
| 4 | "If it has billions and trillions of switches, then it can go up to almost countless numbers." | "**Combined into the billions, these switches allow a computer to represent and manipulate values of almost arbitrary size.**" | Replaces vague conditional with a participle-clause opener — explicit Band-7 grammar showcase. | `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §7, `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §4 |
| 5 | "The reason it matters is because every letter is actually a number, and every emoji is also a number." | "**It is precisely this numerical encoding — of letters, images and even instructions — that turns abstract mathematics into a working machine.**" | Cleft sentence emphasises the mechanism; corrects "the reason… is because" redundancy. | `09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §5, `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §8 |
| 6 | "so that no physical objects such as people or dove needed" | "**so that messages no longer needed to be carried by people or pigeons**" | Fixes missing auxiliary and the singular "dove"; restores natural collocation. | `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §B1, §F1 |
| 7 | "mathematics is inevitable" | "**mathematical reasoning is indispensable**" | Replaces a collocation error with a precise band-7 collocation. | `08-vocabulary-grammar/02-vocabulary-error-correction-bank.md`, `05-band-6-to-7/vocabulary-upgrades.md` |
| 8 | "People have built up mathematical rules by defining new logics to solve problems." | "**Over centuries, mathematicians have built systems of formal logic that turn vague problems into precisely solvable ones.**" | Fixes "new logics" (uncountable), names the mechanism (formal logic), adds defining relative clause. | `09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §A2, `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §2 |
| 9 | "the first ever information which only used electronic signals" | "**arguably the first system to encode language as electrical pulses alone**" | Replaces "information" (wrong noun) with the right concept; hedges with *arguably*. | `08-vocabulary-grammar/02-vocabulary-error-correction-bank.md`, `05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §7 |
| 10 | "By doing that, it can calculate in binary digits." | "**By switching between these two states, the machine performs calculations in binary.**" | Fixes the unclear "that" reference (`05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md`); adds technical precision. | `05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md`, `10-task2-topic-banks/topic-03-technology.md` |

## Overall band rationale
Average 5.25, rounded up to 5.5 per the .25 → .5 rule in `01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md` §3. The vocabulary risk-taking holds LR at 6, but missing thesis + missing conclusion + faulty complex sentences keep TR/CC/GRA at 5 until structural repair lands.

## CONVERGENCE: REFINING
The harness reports an empty prior chain, so per the rules I must output REFINING regardless of confidence. Substantively, future iterations could still tighten the rewrite #5 cleft and reconsider whether LR should drop to 5 once the risk-taker errors are weighed against the genuine topic-vocabulary hits — that boundary call has not yet been stress-tested across iterations.

Doing only this — without changing a single word of vocabulary — would already lift TR and CC to Band 6.
